model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Purdue enters as one of the nation's elite programs, ranked #8 by both KenPom and Torvik with an AdjEM of +30.5 and the nation's top-ranked adjusted offense (AdjOE 132.6 by Torvik) — a level of two-way excellence built against elite Big Ten competition that Queens' ASUN schedule never approached. Queens, making their first-ever NCAA Tournament appearance, carries a negative AdjEM of -1.45 and a defense ranked an alarming 316th nationally, creating a roughly 32-point efficiency gap that is historically insurmountable in a single-elimination game. The Boilermakers arrive in peak form as Big Ten Tournament champions with Braden Smith, Oscar Cluff, and Trey Kaufman-Renn all healthy, while Queens' 21-13 record was built entirely against low-major opposition without a single Power Five win. No credible upset path exists for a first-time tournament qualifier ranked 181st nationally against a top-10 program with the nation's most prolific offense.

reasoningStep.title: Purdue vs. Queens — Round of 64 West Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: A historically lopsided 2-vs-15 matchup. Purdue is a top-10 national program with the nation's top-ranked adjusted offense and a top-36 defense, while Queens is a first-time tournament qualifier with a negative AdjEM and the 316th-ranked defense in Division I. The ~32-point efficiency gap, Purdue's full-strength roster coming off a Big Ten Tournament title, and Queens' complete lack of Power Five competition make this one of the clearest non-1-seed picks in the bracket.
evidence: Purdue: KenPom #8, AdjEM +30.5, AdjOE 131 (#2 nationally by KenPom), AdjDE 100.5 (#36). Queens: KenPom #181, AdjEM -1.45, AdjOE 115.8 (#78), AdjDE 117.3 (#316 nationally). Efficiency gap: ~32 points.

Purdue won the Big Ten Tournament championship over #3 Michigan 80-72. Braden Smith (11 assists, 14 pts in title game), Oscar Cluff (21 pts), Trey Kaufman-Renn all healthy and in peak form. No injury concerns reported.

Queens is making their first-ever NCAA Tournament appearance as ASUN Tournament champions with a 21-13 record. Per CBB Review: Queens did not beat any Power Five teams or mid-majors, solely low-major opposition. Predicted Round of 64 exit by all major analysts.
Purdue is a massive betting favorite, consistent with the ~32-point efficiency gap and the historical 2-vs-15 seed record in the NCAA Tournament.
2-seeds hold an overwhelming historical record against 15-seeds. Queens' 316th-ranked defense against the nation's top offense creates no credible upset scenario.

result.text: Get To Know The 2026 ASUN Tournament Champs: Queens Basketball Skip to content CBB Review (College Basketball Review) College Basketball Power Rankings, Interviews and more! ACC Boston College Cal Clemson Duke Florida State Georgia Tech Louisville Miami (Fla) NC State North Carolina Notre Dame Pitt SMU Stanford Syracuse Virginia Virginia Tech Wake Forest Big East Butler Creighton DePaul Georgetown Marquette Providence Seton Hall St. John’s UConn Villanova Xavier Big Ten Illinois Indiana Iowa Maryland Michigan Michigan State Minnesota Nebraska Northwestern Ohio State Oregon Penn State Purdue Rutgers UCLA USC Washington Wisconsin Big 12 Arizona Arizona State Baylor BYU Cincinnati Colorado Houston Iowa State Kansas Kansas State Oklahoma State TCU Texas Tech UCF Utah West Virginia SEC Alabama Arkansas Auburn Florida Georgia Kentucky LSU Mississippi State Missouri Oklahoma Ole Miss South Carolina Tennessee Texas Texas A&M Vanderbilt Mid Majors AAC Atlantic Sun Atlantic 10 C-USA Horizon MAAC MAC Missouri Valley Mountain West SoCon WAC WCC Oregon State Washington State Bracketology Matty Brackets March on CBB Review Rankings Meet Our Staff Get To Know The 2026 ASUN Tournament Champs: Queens Basketball By georgebagwell Mar 16, 2026 Queens Basketball Logo Queens basketball is making its first NCAA tournament after beating Central Arkansas in a thriller on Sunday. In the first year that Queens basketball was eligible for the NCAA tournament, they made it. Talk about perfect timing. The Royals made the jump from D-II to D-I in 2023, and just three years later, they find themselves in every other team’s shoes, just six games away from winning college basketball’s biggest prize. How they got there: Queens basketball finished the ASUN conference slate with a 13-5 record, after being picked first in the conference’s coaches poll prior to the season. The Royals won four of five in the ASUN regular season, though they lost on the road to Central Arkansas in the regular season finale. They got revenge in an instant classic against the Bears in the championship, winning 98-93 in overtime after beating West Georgia and Austin Peay in the quarterfinals and semifinals, respectively. Three players from Queens basketball were named to the ASUN all-tournament team. Nasir Mann was named tournament MVP, while Chris Ashby and Jordan Watford earned all-tournament honors. General Information: Team: Queens Location: Charlotte, N.C. Record: 21-13 (13-5) Head Coach: Grant Leonard (4th season, all with Queens) What to know about Queens basketball: The Royals are making their first trip to the dance after defeating Central Arkansas 98-93 in overtime in the ASUN Tournament championship game. It’s the first year the program was eligible for the tournament, as they were going through the D-II-to-D-I transition period imposed by the NCAA. Queens is led by a diverse scoring machine: six players currently average double figures for the Royals over the cours

result.text: Purdue vs. Queens University: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Purdue vs. Queens University: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament predictions and preview for Purdue vs. Queens University. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview: Purdue vs. Queens Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. Purdue 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Purdue entered the season with the cursed AP No. 1 ranking, defeating Alabama and Texas Tech before getting trounced by Iowa State in December. The Boilermakers have been one of the most dangerous offensive teams in the country, finishing with the second-best KenPom offense and ranking in the top 25 in offensive rebound rate, turnover rate, two-point shooting rate and three-point shooting rate. Point guard Braden Smith broke the Big Ten’s career assists record this season and is averaging 8.7 per game, only behind Jeremy Fears Jr. nationally. Trey Kaufman-Renn shoots 57% from the field with nearly nine boards per game. Fletcher Loyer has hit over 41% of his threes across the season. Oscar Cluff has been a legitimate steal as a transfer big, shooting 71% from the field and leading the nation in KenPom offensive rating. C.J. Cox provides reliable guard play, with one of the nation’s lowest turnover rates. Daniel Jacobsen, the 7-foot-4 sophomore, gives Purdue a shot-altering presence behind Cluff and Kaufman-Renn that most teams struggle to match. When Purdue is running on all cylinders, it becomes nearly impossible to guard. The defense is where Purdue’s title case gets complicated. The Boilermakers gave up 91 points to Michigan in a home loss that raised some questions. Since that game, Purdue's defense ranks outside of the top 140. Late in the season, the Boilermakers’ defensive breakdowns became consistent enough to create a late-season narrative that has tracked them into the NCAA Tournament. The Royals made the jump from D-II to D-I in 2023, and just three years later, they find themselves in every other team’s shoes, just six games away from winning college basketball’s biggest prize. How they got there: Queens basketball finished the ASUN conference slate with a 13-5 record, after being picked first in the conference’s coaches poll prior to the season. The Royals won four of five in the ASUN regular season, though they lost on the road to Central Arkansas in the regular season finale. They got revenge in an instant classic against the Bears in the championship, winning 98-93 in overtime after beating West Georgia and Austin Peay in the quarterfinals and semifinals, respectively. Three players from Queens basketball were named to the ASUN all-tournament team. Nasir Mann was named tournament MVP, while Chris Ashby and Jordan Watford earned all-tournament honors. General Information: Team: Queens Location: Charlotte, N.C. Record: 21-13 (13-5) Head Coach: Grant Leonard (4th season, all with Queens) What to know about Queens basketball: The Royals are making their first trip to the dance after defeating Central Arkansas 98-93 in overtime in the ASUN Tournament championship game. It’s the first year the program was eligible for the tournament, as they were going through the D-II-to-D-I transition period imposed by the NCAA. The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament predictions and preview for Purdue vs. Queens University. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview: Purdue vs. Queens Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. Purdue 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Purdue entered the season with the cursed AP No. 1 ranking, defeating Alabama and Texas Tech before getting trounced by Iowa State in December. The Boilermakers have been one of the most dangerous offensive teams in the country, finishing with the second-best KenPom offense and ranking in the top 25 in offensive rebound rate, turnover rate, two-point shooting rate and three-point shooting rate. Point guard Braden Smith broke the Big Ten’s career assists record this season and is averaging 8.7 per game, only behind Jeremy Fears Jr. nationally. Trey Kaufman-Renn shoots 57% from the field with nearly nine boards per game. Fletcher Loyer has hit over 41% of his threes across the season. Oscar Cluff has been a legitimate steal as a transfer big, shooting 71% from the field and leading the nation in KenPom offensive rating. C.J. Cox provides reliable guard play, with one of the nation’s lowest turnover rates. Daniel Jacobsen, the 7-foot-4 sophomore, gives Purdue a shot-altering presence behind Cluff and Kaufman-Renn that most teams struggle to match. When Purdue is running on all cylinders, it becomes nearly impossible to guard. The defense is where Purdue’s title case gets complicated. The Boilermakers gave up 91 points to Michigan in a home loss that raised some questions. Since that game, Purdue's defense ranks outside of the top 140. Late in the season, the Boilermakers’ defensive breakdowns became consistent enough to create a late-season narrative that has tracked them into the NCAA Tournament. While Purdue doesn’t allow many second-chance

Queens, making their first-ever NCAA Tournament appearance, carries a negative AdjEM of -1.45 and a defense ranked an alarming 316th nationally, creating a roughly 32-point efficiency gap that is historically insurmountable in a single-elimination game. The Boilermakers arrive in peak form as Big Ten Tournament champions with Braden Smith, Oscar Cluff, and Trey Kaufman-Renn all healthy, while Queens' 21-13 record was built entirely against low-major opposition without a single Power Five win.
